communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is narrowed request relative to my first request about the same matter. Narrowed Request: For dates January 1, 2022 to February 28, 2026, I request all correspondence between EEOC and Opexus management and staff - including staff and management from AINS, which is the former company name of Opexus.

Background:
On March 3, 2026, I responded to EEOC's February 26, 2026 acknowledgment notice (or as stated by EEOC to be on February 27, 2026) and stated: "Regarding # 820-2026-005851, I thank you for the response. I do not have an account with EEOC's FOIA Office. As a standing request, ask that EEOC not require me to log into a potentially compromised FOIA system to view any updates , etc. This is especially salient because of recent serious security concerns.... Thank you for considering my requests and desire for secure and transparent communications." Again I do not have an account with EEOC's FOIA portal and I elect not to use it for reasons I have explained. I request that all communications occur outside of the EEOC FOIA platform and be transparently sent for public view.

On April 9, 2026, EEOC Office of Legal Counsel sent a letter stating among other things, "This letter is to inform you that we would like to have a conversation with you in hopes of clarifying and narrowing your request. We conducted a search on February 27, 2026, based on your request as written, which resulted in over 124,000 items. We do not have an exact page count, but assuming that doing an industry standard of about three (3)pages per item, the actual page count is going to be likely around the neighborhood of over 372,000 pages. We are putting this request on hold so that we may have a conversation with you concerning how you can effectively limit the scope of this request, so that it can be processed. " Unfortunately EEOC only provided me with 10 days to respond. I was on annual leave during these 10 days. Thus, I am resubmitting this modified request.

On May 10, 2026, I resubmitted a narrow request resulting in a May 28, 2026 rejection. Please consider my reasonable accommodation request of at least 20 days to respond to any notices through the MuckRock portal. Also, I ask that in the future, that you not close any of my requests if I do not respond in less than 20 days. This is a standing request. Phone calls and access to the EEOC FOIA portal are not options for me. 

I will always remain interested in receiving all requests until they are received.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessica Elm
